title: MBA DTOL - DNA extraction and barcoding of Marine Metazoans
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"36rd0","text":"This document provides a guide to the protocols used by the Marine Biological Association for DNA extraction and barcoding of marine metazoans sampled for the Darwin Tree of Life project. The selection of organisms described are due to these species requiring more optimized techniques than the high-throughput method otherwise used by the Natural History Museum (dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8epv5x7qjg1b/v1). Please note that this is a working document, with more organism-specific barcoding methods being added as they become optimized. The methods described are generally successful for the selected groups, but may still require further optimization for some samples. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":364,"length":48}],"data":{}},{"key":"1ccnc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"0D15A53C849311EE97E90A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8epv5x7qjg1b/v1"}}}}
steps: Reference: Adapted from Salonna et al., 2021 Salonna, M., Gasparini, F., Huchon, D., Montesanto, F., Haddas-Sasson, M., Ekins, M., McNamara, M., Mastrototaro, F. and Gissi, C., 2021. An elongated COI fragment to discriminate botryllid species and as an improved ascidian DNA barcode. Scientific reports , 11 (1), p.4078. Primers: COI barcode %s %s F dinF CGTTGRTTTATRTCTACWAATCATAARGA R Nux1R GCAGTAAAATAWGCTCGRGARTC PCR reaction mix: %s %s Mastermix 1x GoTaq 5x buffer 10ul MgCl2 (25mM) 4ul dNTPs (10uM each) 1ul Primer (10uM) 2ul (each) Mol. H2O 29.75ul Taq 0.25ul DNA template 1ul (usually 1:10 dil. of extracts) Total 50ul Thermocycling conditions: 95C, 3mins, 30x[95C, 30s; 50C, 30s; 72C, 1.5, mins], 72C, 5mins If unsuccessful, try repeating PCR with x35 cycles instead (generally required)

Reference: Adapted from Moran et al., 2016 Moran, Z., Orth, D.J., Schmitt, J.D., Hallerman, E.M. and Aguilar, R., 2016. Effectiveness of DNA barcoding for identifying piscine prey items in stomach contents of piscivorous catfishes. Environmental Biology of Fishes , 99 , pp.161-167. Primer: COI barcode %s %s F FishF2_t1 TGTAAAACGACGGCCAGTCGACTAATCATAAAGATATCGGCAC R FishR2_t1 CAGGAAACAGCTATGACACTTCAGGGTGACCGAAGAATCAGAA PCR reaction mix %s %s Mastermix 1x GoTaq 5x buffer 5ul MgCl2 (25mM) 3ul dNTPs (10uM each) 0.625ul Primer (10uM) 0.25ul (each) Mol. H2O 14ul Taq 0.125ul DNA template 2ul Total 25ul Thermocycling conditions: 94C, 2mins, 35x[94C, 30s; 52C, 40s; 72C, 1min], 72C, 10min

References: Adapted from Beckman et al., 2023 and Cunningham & Buss, 1993 Beckmann, L.M., Soto-Angel, J.J., Hosia, A. and Martell, L., 2023. Odd family reunion: DNA barcoding reveals unexpected relationship between three hydrozoan species. PeerJ , 11 , p.e15118. Cunningham, C.W. and Buss, L.W., 1993. Molecular evidence for multiple episodes of paedomorphosis in the family Hydractiniidae. Biochemical Systematics and Ecology , 21 (1), pp.57-69. DNA extraction: Left in heat block for 1.5hrs (exoskeleton remains in tube – does not mean DNA is not extracted!) Relatively varied DNA concentrations - usually required 1:10 dilution (if >10ng/ul) to reduce contamination. Primers: 16s barcode %s %s F SHA ACGGAATGAACTCAAATCATGT 600bp R SHB TCGACTGTTTACCAAAAACATA 600bp PCR reaction mix: %s %s Mastermix 1x RedTaq with MgCl2 25ul Primer (10uM) 1ul Mol. H2O 22ul DNA template 1ul (usually 1:10 dil. of extracts) Total 50ul Thermocycling conditions: 94C,5mins, 35x[94C, 30s; 50C, 30s; 72C, 1mins], 72C, 7 mins If failed, increase reaction above to x39 cycles (some may better with x35 some better with x39 due to extent of contaminants)

*Note: This method has been optimized for barnacle barcoding, but may also be useful for other crustaceans as universal COI primers are used (this has not been trialled). Reference: Adapted from Benny et al., 2007 Chan, B.K., Tsang, L.M. and Chu, K.H., 2007. Morphological and genetic differentiation of the acorn barnacle Tetraclita squamosa (Crustacea, Cirripedia) in East Asia and description of a new species of Tetraclita. Zoologica Scripta , 36 (1), pp.79-91. Primer: COI barcodes %s %s F LCO1409 GGTCAACAAATCATAAAGATATTGG 600bp R HCO2198 TAAACTTCAGGGTGACCAAAAAATCA 600bp PCR reaction mix: %s %s Mastermix 1x GoTaq 5x buffer 10ul MgCl2 (25mM) 4ul dNTPs (10uM each) 1ul Primer (10uM) 1ul (each) Mol. H2O 31.7ul Taq 0.3ul DNA template 1ul Total 50ul Thermocycling conditions: 94C,3mins, 33x[94C, 30s; 47C, 30s; 72C, 40s], 72C, 3 mins

References: Adapted from Figueroa et al, 2020 and Rossel et al., 2023 Figueroa, N.J., Figueroa, D.F. and Hicks, D., 2020. Phylogeography of Acartia tonsa Dana, 1849 (Calanoida: Copepoda) and phylogenetic reconstruction of the genus Acartia Dana, 1846. Marine Biodiversity , 50 , pp.1-20. Rossel, S., Kaiser, P., Bode‐Dalby, M., Renz, J., Laakmann, S., Auel, H., Hagen, W., Arbizu, P.M. and Peters, J., 2023. Proteomic fingerprinting enables quantitative biodiversity assessments of species and ontogenetic stages in Calanus congeners (Copepoda, Crustacea) from the Arctic Ocean. Molecular Ecology Resources , 23 (2), pp.382-395. DNA extraction: Samples were incubated in 30 μl chelex (InstaGene Matrix, Bio-Rad) for 50 min at 56°C, followed by a denaturation of the enzymes for 10 min at 96°C. Centrifuge samples @10,000rpm for 1min and carefully pipette out supernatant (avoid Chelex beads!). The resulting sample was used directly as DNA template for PCR amplification. Sample concentrations varied between 1-50ng/ul. Primers: COI barcode %s %s F LCO1409 GGTCAACAAATCATAAAGATATTGG 600bp R HCO2198 TAAACTTCAGGGTGACCAAAAAATCA 600bp PCR reaction mix: %s %s Mastermix 1x GoTaq 5x buffer 10ul MgCl2 (25mM) 4ul dNTPs (10uM each) 1ul BSA (20mg/ml) 1ul Primer (10uM) 2ul (each) Mol. H2O 24.75ul Taq 0.25ul DNA template 5ul Total 50ul Thermocycling conditions: 94C, 5mins, 38x[94C, 45s; 42C, 45s; 72C, 1,20s], 72C, 7 min

DNA extraction: Unless otherwise stated, DNA extraction for marine metazoans is done with the Qiagen DNAeasy Mouse Blood and Tissue kit. https://www.qiagen.com/us/Resources/ResourceDetail?id=aa250d94-fc4b-4e27-bb74-d32391ff8a48&lang=en The provided protocol is followed, apart from the final elution step: Pre-heated elution buffer (56C) is used to increase yield. An elution volume of 60ul is used to increase the final DNA concentration. The buffer is left to incubate on the membrane for 5 minutes. If sample yields are relatively high (>25ng/ul), gDNA samples are usually diluted by 1:10 to reduce contamination during PCR amplification.
creator: Rebekka Uhl
